visual_differentiation_axes: regal war-table dashboard instead of top-pill plus bottom-strip HUD
royal blue and gold medieval map palette instead of neon or wasteland tones
left doctrine card and right alert rail framing the battlefield without overlap
heraldic brass and parchment material language
crowned gatehouse over a split river-crossing campaign board instead of pennant-led crest icons
forbidden_visual_reuse: top HUD pill cluster plus bottom command strip
heavy frame over active map routes

ui_identity.layout_archetype: regal war-table dashboard with top command ribbon, left doctrine card, right alert rail, and bottom-right tactical cluster
ui_identity.hud_composition: troop reserve, command points, province objective, timer, current doctrine, alert queue, tactical cooldowns, and selected-node summary
ui_identity.navigation_model: menu to province map to battle briefing to gameplay, with compact pause and battle result overlays plus a separate realm-upgrade screen
ui_identity.palette_signature: royal blue, parchment tan, iron steel, old gold, muted forest green, and enemy crimson
ui_identity.material_language: heraldic military tablecraft with wax seals, brass bezels, stitched map leather, enamel badges, and engraved route markers
ui_identity.typography_style: serif campaign titles with condensed tactical labels and large numeric battlefield counters

reserved_edges: bottom-right 22 by 24 percent for tactical cluster
hud_anchor_zones: top left troop reserve and command points
playfield_safety.frame_overlay_policy: decorative brass trims and parchment corners must stay in reserved dead space and may not overlap roads, nodes, troop streams, or targeting paths

unique_screen_motifs: realm upgrade chamber styled like a council ledger
victory screen as a campaign chronicle page
forbidden_ui_elements: floating frame over the map core

icon_identity.subject: a crowned stone gatehouse above a split river bridge on a brass campaign map with etched route pins below
icon_identity.silhouette: broad arched gatehouse with a floating crown crest, one broken bridge span, and a low curved river-road band
icon_identity.composition: the gatehouse fills the upper center, the crown hovers just above the battlements, a broken bridge cut creates negative space in the lower half, and etched brass route pins guide the eye across the river map
icon_identity.palette: deep royal blue, iron gray, old gold, parchment glow, and a sharp crimson enemy accent
icon_identity.background: soft parchment-lit dusk gradient without a generic shield medallion or circular badge reuse
icon_identity.game_specific_motif: the icon communicates contested crossings and strategic city-state warfare through a fortified royal gate and broken bridge map cue
forbidden_icon_reuse: unmodified icon export reused from another project
